mechanism: On a vocab_gap for a candidate-NEW grammar token, the token is embedded (free local Encoder) and compared by cosine vs its segment's registered vocabulary. A near-hit >= threshold chains a compose retry asking the agent to reuse-or-confirm. A reused token DISAPPEARS from result.vocab_gaps (no VocabGap written); a re-emitted token is stamped distinct_confirmed. Never a hard reject.

protocol.note: --names-only used because the PRIMARY signal is the compose axis (vocab_gap token count + reused/distinct stamps + retries fired); docs are the expensive paid leg that stranded the prior self-refine trial. Both arms ran identical paths/caps. focus mode auto-resets each arm from scratch.

flagged_examples: 10:04:27 — 2/3 tokens flagged as likely synonyms → retry
10:08:13 — 1/1 flagged → routed to grammar B2 retry (normalized_of_parallel_wave_power)
10:08:55 — 1/1 flagged → gap physical_base:mass_flow skipped as decomposable (0 nodes written)
ON.cost_note: ON paid-total ($0.79) < OFF ($2.95) is a THROUGHPUT CONFOUND, not a win: the 4 token-reuse retries added local compose latency, throttling name flow into the paid review/refine pools within the same 16-min cap (OFF reviewed 13, ON 5). Same lesson as the self-refine trial — cost delta under a time cap is not a clean signal.

verdict: KEEP ON by default at threshold 0.85. The dedup check works exactly as designed and is the good outcome from the self-refine memo's framing: it is ADVISORY (re-prompt, never a hard reject), so it does NOT degrade name quality the way the self-refine reject-gate did. In the ON arm it fired on 5 candidate-new tokens and folded ALL of them — minting ZERO new VocabGap synonym nodes (the primary metric: dedup should REDUCE distinct gap tokens, and it did, to zero net-new). No distinct_confirmed survivors means nothing genuinely distinct was wrongly suppressed in this cohort. Name quality did not degrade: ON names (pressure_at_inlet, temperature_at_inlet, parallel_electrical_conductivity, reference_vacuum_magnetic_field, power_density_at_midplane) are clean — in several cases SHORTER/cleaner than OFF (OFF over-qualified: coolant_pressure_at_inlet, maximum_heat_flux_at_wall). The dedup fold actively trimmed redundant qualifiers.
caveats: Synonym-fold opportunities are RARE on physics-quantity cohorts: most paths compose from already-registered tokens, so the check fired only 4 times across 32 paths. The benefit accrues over MANY runs (slow vocab accretion), not on any single cohort — gap-count reduction here is 1→0 net-new.

Name-quality A/B is UNDERPOWERED: the time cap + compose-latency-from-retries throttled the ON arm to 5 reviewed names (OFF 13). The compose-axis signal (folds, zero new gaps) is the headline and is clean; the accept-score comparison is secondary and thin. No quality REGRESSION was observed.
Cost delta is confounded by throughput under the time cap — not evidence either way on spend.

focus mode mutates prior-arm SN nodes on reset (sets them to pending under the new run_id), so the OFF arm's clean stage/score snapshot had to be captured BEFORE launching ON. Recorded methodology note for future A/Bs using --focus.
recommended_threshold: 0.85 (keep default). The check is advisory so a moderately permissive threshold is safe — it surfaces more reuse candidates without risk of hard-rejecting distinct concepts. Lowering toward the module's DUPLICATE_THRESHOLD=0.82 would surface a few more folds at negligible extra cost (free local embeddings); 0.85 is a reasonable conservative default. No case for raising it.
graph_cleanup: 52 scratch StandardName nodes (+Review/DocsRevision children) deleted; 32 cohort sources reset to extracted; 0 residual SN reachable; 0 stray run_ids. VocabGap preserved at 70 (69 pre-existing + 1 genuine device/coil gap, NOT dedup-attributable).
